manuals.source_name: Day1_实验手册1-安装配置虚拟机.docx
manuals.purpose: Configure VMware Workstation and install Ubuntu 20.04 as the standard operating environment for all subsequent experiments.
preparation: Windows host computer with sufficient memory and disk space.
procedure: Install VMware Workstation and verify that the new virtual-machine wizard is available.
Create an Ubuntu virtual machine with appropriate CPU, memory, disk, and NAT network settings.
Install Ubuntu, perform first-boot setup, install VMware Tools, and create a clean snapshot.
verification: Confirm network access from Ubuntu.
Verify screen scaling and host-to-guest file transfer.
Restore the clean snapshot once to confirm recovery is available.

manuals.purpose: Install ROS and establish the basic concepts used by later robot-control experiments.
preparation: Terminal access and basic Linux command familiarity.
procedure: Install ROS using the documented installation path or classroom script.
Start roscore and run turtlesim-related commands.
Use ROS tools to inspect nodes, topics, graphs, and message data.
verification: Explain package, node, topic, publisher, and subscriber roles.
Run turtlesim and teleoperation successfully.
Use rqt_graph, rqt_plot, rosnode, and rostopic for observation.

manuals.zh_title: 初次配置与驱动 Crazyflie 无人机
manuals.source_name: Day1_实验手册3-初次配置与驱动crazyfile无人机.docx
manuals.purpose: Prepare the Crazyflie software and hardware connection path for safe first operation.
preparation: Ubuntu/ROS environment from the previous manuals.
Crazyflie aircraft, battery, USB cable, and Crazyradio.
cflib, Crazyflie client, and required Python dependencies.
procedure: Install the Crazyflie client and required libraries.
Configure USB permissions and radio connection settings.
Connect to the aircraft and run conservative first-control tests.
verification: Crazyflie client starts correctly.
USB and radio connection can detect the aircraft.
A short scripted test can be explained and stopped safely.

manuals.purpose: Read multi-direction distance measurements and connect sensor readings to basic flight behavior.
preparation: Clear indoor test area with simple obstacles.
procedure: Connect to Crazyflie through cflib.
Read front, back, left, right, up, and down range values.
Use threshold rules to generate simple reactive movement.
verification: Range values change consistently when obstacles move.
Students can identify which sensor direction triggered a response.
The reactive behavior remains inside the safety area.

manuals.purpose: Extend basic range reading into mission rules and obstacle-aware flight actions.
procedure: Collect range readings under several obstacle configurations.
Implement obstacle-triggered actions such as landing, backing away, or bouncing.
Adjust thresholds and speeds conservatively.
verification: The program exits through a safe stop condition.
Sensor logs support the observed behavior.
The selected thresholds can be justified from the measurements.

manuals.source_name: Day2_实验手册3-复杂地图飞行与建图.docx
manuals.purpose: Use controlled flight and range sensing to explore a more complex obstacle map.
preparation: Mapping or logging script from the manual.
procedure: Inspect the map constraints and permitted flight area.
Run the flight or mapping procedure slowly enough for stable data collection.
Review the generated obstacle points or map-like output.
verification: The flight remains within the allowed area.
The map output reflects major walls or obstacle regions.
Failure cases are documented with likely causes.

manuals.source_name: Day2_实验手册4-自主建图+复习.docx
manuals.purpose: Consolidate the ranging and mapping workflow through an autonomous mapping review task.
preparation: Known test area and flight restrictions.
procedure: Review connection, sensing, control, and mapping requirements.
Design or modify a route that samples useful map areas.
Run the autonomous mapping task and record the result.
verification: The route is reproducible.
The produced map or log supports the route explanation.
Main risks and improvements are recorded objectively.

manuals.source_name: Day3_实验手册1-路径规划仿真.docx
manuals.purpose: Run path-planning simulation experiments and compare route-generation behavior.
preparation: Terminal access in the project directory.
procedure: Prepare and build the simulation project as documented.
Set start and goal conditions in the simulator or visualization tool.
Run A*, RRT, RRT*, or the provided planning examples and observe outputs.
verification: A route is generated for the selected task.
The observed route can be compared across algorithms or parameters.
Execution notes include commands, errors, and screenshots where applicable.

manuals.purpose: Use cflib to structure Crazyflie connection, logging, parameter access, and command scripts.
preparation: Known radio URI or scanning procedure.
procedure: Initialize CRTP drivers and create a Crazyflie object.
Use synchronized connection patterns for safer program structure.
Read parameters or logs and send controlled commands.
verification: The script connects and exits cleanly.
Logged or printed values match the expected aircraft state.
Motion commands are short, conservative, and explainable.

manuals.source_name: Day3_实验手册3-Motion Commander进阶编程.docx
manuals.purpose: Use Motion Commander movement primitives to construct repeatable flight routines.
procedure: Create a MotionCommander context.
Combine takeoff, landing, directional movement, turns, and pauses.
Test short movement segments before a complete sequence.
verification: The drone completes the intended primitive sequence.
Timing, height, and distance choices are recorded.
The program can be stopped safely if behavior deviates.

manuals.source_name: Day3_实验手册4-阶段综合实践.docx
manuals.purpose: Combine setup, sensing, planning, and control skills in a bounded practical task.
preparation: Team role assignment for operation, monitoring, and recording.
procedure: Read the task constraints and define success criteria.
Break the route or behavior into testable components.
Run the integrated task and record results.
verification: The task is completed within the defined constraints.
The team can explain the selected method.
Problems are documented with concrete evidence.

manuals.purpose: Use coordinate-based commands for waypoint routes and structured spatial tasks.
preparation: Crazyflie setup with appropriate positioning support.
procedure: Initialize PositionCommander or the documented position-control API.
Set default height, speed, and coordinate frame carefully.
Execute waypoint routes such as square, cube, star, or map-based paths.
verification: The coordinate route matches the intended geometry.
The aircraft remains inside the allowed volume.
The route can be adjusted by editing explicit coordinates.

manuals.source_name: Day3_实验手册6-综合项目展示任务.docx
manuals.purpose: Present a complete project task using the course experiments as technical basis.
preparation: Working code and tested hardware setup.
Presentation evidence such as logs, route sketches, video, or screenshots.
procedure: Select a feasible demonstration task.
Implement and rehearse the workflow under safety constraints.
Present the method, result, limitations, and improvement plan.
verification: The demonstration is executable or reproducible.
The presentation explains method and evidence objectively.
Safety, teamwork, and failure handling are included in the report.
